seoTitle: How to Use Webhooks in Zendesk to Push Live Data
metaDescription: Learn how to use webhooks in Zendesk to connect triggers and automations to external endpoints like Slack or your CRM with secure, event-driven pushes.
intro: Constantly polling an API for updates is inefficient and hard to maintain. Zendesk's native webhooks dashboard lets you push data instantly to external tools like Slack, a CRM, or a backend database whenever a ticket event happens. This guide covers creating a webhook, formatting the payload, and wiring it into a trigger.

steps.heading: Open the Webhooks panel
steps.body: In Admin Center, go to Apps and Integrations and click Webhooks to see your existing connections.

steps.heading: Create a new webhook
steps.body: Click Create Webhook and choose whether it will be triggered by a Ticket Trigger, Automation, or a direct system Event.

steps.heading: Enter the destination URL
steps.body: Give the webhook a descriptive name and paste the external third-party endpoint URL it should send data to.

steps.heading: Configure the request format
steps.body: Set the request method to POST and select JSON as your request format.

steps.heading: Add authentication
steps.body: Choose an authentication method, such as a secure bearer token or basic API key, to protect the endpoint.

steps.heading: Bind it to a trigger and test
steps.body: Open Objects and Rules to bind the webhook to a trigger or automation action, then run a live test ticket and check the activity logs.

faq.question: What's the difference between binding to a trigger versus a system event?
faq.answer: A trigger fires on specific ticket conditions you define, while system events push data based on broader platform-level occurrences.

faq.question: How do I know if my webhook actually fired?
faq.answer: The webhooks activity logs record invocation history and any retry codes, letting you confirm delivery.

faq.question: Can I secure the webhook endpoint?
faq.answer: Yes, Zendesk supports basic and bearer token authentication for outgoing webhook requests.

faq.question: Does this replace the need for API polling?
faq.answer: For most real-time use cases, yes — webhooks push updates immediately instead of requiring scheduled API polling.
